## Summary

Focused UI update for `/workbench-v2`: make `ReviseCockpitClient` the active fixed-viewport cockpit.

## Scope

In scope: compact top bar, left rail filters, direct Accept A/B/C buttons, collapsible ledger, independent queue and center scrolling, existing decision sync, and auto-advance.

Out of scope: evaluation architecture, TrustedPath, revision opportunity ledger writer ownership, data pipeline logic, and branding redesign.

## Branch Freshness (Never Behind)

Branch-Behind-Base: 0

## Visual Evidence

Screenshots showed the prior page still had a large report header, document scrolling, and a bottom ledger. This PR replaces that with a cockpit layout. Manual 1366x768 browser verification remains required before merge.

## Accessibility

Native buttons remain used for primary controls. No intentional keyboard trap is added. Queue and center panel scroll independently.

## Browser Targets

Chromium-based browsers, Firefox, and Safari.

## Risks & Anomalies

UI-only change. Main risk is viewport behavior on smaller laptops. No new background job or evaluation pipeline path is introduced.

## Unauthorized Input Sources

None added. UI reads the existing workbench queue payload and the existing revision ledger API only.

## Internal Process Leakage

No internal workflow or pipeline details are surfaced in user-visible UI copy.

## Input → Action → Output

Input: existing workbench queue payload. Action: author chooses Accept A/B/C, Keep, Reject, Defer, or Write Custom. Output: decision state is sent to the revision ledger API and persisted in revision ledger decisions.

## Public-Safe Quality/Status Metrics

Visible metrics are limited to queue position, priority counts, pending, accepted, and sync state. No internal quality gate diagnostics are exposed.

## Runtime/Pipeline Expansion

No new runtime jobs. No new background stages. No evaluation stage behavior altered. No workbench page-load ledger writer introduced.

## Latency Impact

No-Pipeline-Impact: true

UI-only change. No new server pipeline path introduced. No evaluation pass timing is changed.

## Tests

Required before merge: npm run build, authenticated workbench v2 smoke check, 1366x768 viewport check, and decision persistence check.
